mission.title: kubevela: [Feature] Add support for a trait to patch to any outputs defined before it and not just the output defined by the corresponding component.
mission.description: [Feature] Add support for a trait to patch to any outputs defined before it and not just the output defined by the corresponding component.. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kubevela deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubevela version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
helm list -n kubevela 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevela installation.

steps.title: Review kubevela configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevela configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get configmap -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevela
```
Currently, traits can patch the workload of the corresponding components only. It is not possible for traits to patch workloads of other traits. This tends to make a trait overly complex when its targeting a fairly complex resource. For e.g.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] Add support for a trait to patch to any outputs…
steps.description:
As  said, the first point, also is the main point of this issue, is already implemented in the form of `patch: context: outputs: resourcename`. However, the `context` here can override the global context in our cue files, so I'll submit a PR to fix that.
As for the second point, I think we can
```yaml
apiVersion: core.oam.dev/v1beta1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: sample-service
spec:
  components:
    - name: myapp
      type: webservice
      properties:
        image: myregistry.io/projectid/sample-service
        port: 8000
      traits:
        - type: readiness-probes
          properties:
            http:
              path: /health
              port: 8090
              interval: 20s
        # These are the traits of concern
        - type: service-mesh
          properties:
            enabled: true
        - type: mesh-routes
          properties:
            routes:
       
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get events -n kubevela --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Add support for a trait to patch to any outputs…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
As  said, the first point, also is the main point of this issue, is already implemented in the form of `patch: context: outputs: resourcename`. However, the `context` here can override the global context in our cue files, so I'll submit a PR to fix that.
As for the second point, I think we can discuss that in another issue, and this issue can be closed when my PR is merged.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: core.oam.dev/v1beta1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: sample-service
spec:
  components:
    - name: myapp
      type: webservice
      properties:
        image: myregistry.io/projectid/sample-service
        port: 8000
      traits:
        - type: readiness-probes
          properties:
            http:
              path: /health
              port: 8090
              interval: 20s
        # These are the traits of concern
        - type: service-mesh
          properties:
            enabled: true
        - type: mesh-routes
          properties:
            routes:
               - name: "api-routes"
                 match:
                   - uri:
                       prefix: "/v1/api"
        - type: loadbalancing-policy
          properties:
            policy: R

# This resource is created by the service mesh trait
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  hosts:
  - myapp
  http:
  - name: "api-routes"                 # This route is generated from the mesh-routes trait
    match:
    - uri:
        prefix: "/v1/api"
    route:
    - destination:
        host: myapp

# The resource is defined via the service mesh trait
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  host: myapp
  trafficPolicy:
    loadBalancer:                          #This part is generated from the loadbalancing trait
      simple: ROUND_ROBIN
    connectionPool:                        #This part is generated from the circuit breaking trait
      tcp:
        maxConnections: 100

apiVersion: core.oam.dev/v1beta1
kind: TraitDefinition
metadata:
  name: patch
spec:
  schematic:
    cue:
      template: |
        patch: {spec: template: metadata: labels: app: context.name}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevela installed or the issue environment reproducible.
